prompt: I want to create a SaaS product login page, show me 3 style directions for comparison
expected: Loads the base-required bundle first; asks only the route-shaping questions still needed for context/constraints; routes to variant-exploration for 3 directions; avoids purple gradients/emoji/Inter and other AI slop; provides specific reasoning for each variation's differentiation dimension
tests: base-required bundle + route-shaping questions + variations logic + anti-AI slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me make a 10-page product pitch deck for an AI tool startup
expected: Starts with deck_stage.js; verbally vocalizes design system (colors/fonts/layout rhythm) and waits for confirmation; alternates section divider/content/data/quote layouts; font size ≥24px; 1-indexed labels
tests: Junior Designer: report first then execute + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale compliance

prompt: Create a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work
expected: Uses animations.jsx Stage+Sprite; writes timeline first then components; entry easeOut exit easeIn; tells story in phases not stacking animations; text dwell ≥3s
tests: animations workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Create a Habit Tracker App prototype
expected: Asks user for overview flat layout or flow demo (defaults to overview); uses assets/ios_frame.jsx, doesn't hand-write Dynamic Island; Tracker is high-density type, each screen ≥3 information density elements (completion rate, streak days, trend curve, achievement badge, etc., not decorative); at least 5-7 screens side-by-side (home/new habit/detail/stats/settings)
tests: overview/flow form routing + ios_frame hard binding + information density classification (high-density) + multi-screen side-by-side

prompt: Create a Reading Notes App prototype
expected: Overview flat layout primary; ios_frame.jsx; reading notes leans content-display, density requirements less extreme than Tracker, but notes list still needs ≥3 information layers (book, quote, tags, progress); at least 4-6 screens (home bookshelf/note detail/highlight annotation/search/notebook management); font priority serif display
tests: overview default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-focused visual rhythm

prompt: Create a Running Tracker App prototype
expected: Overview flat layout; ios_frame.jsx; running app is high-density type (map, pace curve, heart rate zones, per-km split data), each screen ≥3 product-differentiating info elements; at least 5 screens (today overview/real-time during run/route map/history/monthly stats); avoids AI slop (no purple gradients, no decorative icon stacking, but data visualization icons allowed)
tests: overview + ios_frame + high-density data visualization + map/chart mixed layout + slop boundary conditions

prompt: Design a fintech landing page for SMB payroll automation
expected: Loads the base-required bundle first; asks only the missing route-shaping questions that affect bundle choice; routes to landing-page; once enough context exists, outputs a visible execution plan with confirmed facts, assumptions, first artifact, variation axes, and verification approach; waits for approval before building HTML
tests: two-stage routing + planning gate + visible plan + approval before build

prompt: Build a polished onboarding flow for a B2B AI sales assistant. Audience is RevOps managers. Three screens. Use a restrained enterprise tone. Just move fast.
expected: Skips most clarification because the brief is rich and speed is requested, but still produces a compressed mini-plan before building; does not jump directly into HTML unless the user explicitly says to skip planning
tests: rich-brief path + speed path + mini-plan still required

prompt: Create a Stripe-style pricing page and export it as PDF
expected: Loads the base-required bundle first; routes to brand-style-clone + landing-page; includes before-export for the PDF target; asks only for missing brand/context details before planning; keeps export constraints visible before build
tests: brand clone + landing page + export checkpoint + two-stage routing
